title: c: cite sentinel — formalize KB: none reason enums + doctor cite-coverage breakdown column
scope: bootstrap template + AGENTS.md cite policy + doctor cite-coverage renderer
description: Current cite policy accepts `KB: none` as a bare answer, conflating 'no relevant rule exists' (legitimate gap) with 'didn't bother to search' (user-discipline issue). Formalize two sentinel reasons — `KB: none [no-relevant]` (LLM searched, nothing applicable) and `KB: none [not-applicable]` (rule exists but doesn't fit current context). Bare `KB: none` is mapped to `[unspecified]` for legacy/lazy emissions and surfaced in the doctor cite-coverage report as a separate breakdown column.

files.change: Document two sentinel reasons + show example KB: none [no-relevant] / KB: none [not-applicable]; mark bare `KB: none` as `[unspecified]` legacy form
Mirror the bootstrap rewrite — new sentinel enums in cite policy
Add sentinel breakdown column: cited / dismissed / none-no-relevant / none-not-applicable / none-unspecified

implementation: Update bootstrap template + .fabric/AGENTS.md cite policy block (note this overlaps with TASK-001 — coordinate string edits)
Update cite-coverage event-parsing regex to capture the optional [reason] sentinel tail
Extend renderCiteCoverageReport to include new breakdown columns
Backfill: unit-test sentinel parser for all five forms
Update fab_review or related reviewer prompts that quote cite-policy text

reference.examples: Mirror dismissed_reason enum extraction pattern from rc.21

criteria: Bootstrap + AGENTS.md teach both sentinel reasons explicitly
Sentinel parser handles all five forms including bare legacy `KB: none`
Unit tests pass for parser + renderer

rationale.chosen_approach: Enum sentinels with legacy bare-form fallback to `[unspecified]` — preserves existing emissions while creating discipline path forward
alternatives_considered: Hard-require sentinel (rejected: would block legacy event streams)
Free-form reason text (rejected: lower aggregation value)
rationale.tradeoffs: Parser slightly more complex (5-way) — small cost

manual_checks: Run `fab doctor --cite-coverage --since=7d` and confirm new breakdown columns appear
success_metrics: ≥80% of new KB: none emissions carry a sentinel within first week of rc.23
